chunk_summary: This section discusses the initiation of postpyloric enteral nutrition with numeric prescription targets for days 0–3, focusing on advancing nutrition delivery in the context of enteral and parenteral nutrition strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of initiating postpyloric enteral nutrition.
Learn numeric advancement targets for enteral nutrition during days 0–3.
Identify key considerations for postpyloric feeding in clinical practice.
key_facts: Postpyloric feeding bypasses the stomach, delivering nutrition directly to the small intestine.
Day 0–3 advancement targets are critical for achieving early nutritional goals.
Enteral nutrition is preferred over parenteral nutrition when the gastrointestinal tract is functional.
Initiation of postpyloric feeding may reduce the risk of aspiration in high-risk patients.
reference_quote: Initiation of postpyloric feeding is recommended for patients at risk of aspiration or with delayed gastric emptying.
